business_goal: Allow registered users to securely access their account.
scope: Allow users to log in using registered email and password.
Implement the 'Forgot Password' flow via email link.
out_of_scope: Login via social media accounts (Google, Facebook, etc.).

functional_requirements.description: The system must allow users to log in by providing a registered email address and password.
The system must validate the provided credentials against the stored user records.
Upon successful login, the system must generate and return a secure authentication token (e.g., JWT) to the client.
The system must provide a 'Forgot Password' mechanism, initiating a password reset process via a secure email link.

non_functional_requirements.description: The login API response time must be fast, ideally under 500ms, even under peak load.
The login interface must be fully responsive and usable across major screen sizes (desktop, tablet, mobile).
Error messages (e.g., invalid credentials, network issues) must be clear, actionable, and non-technical.

user_stories.goal: I want to log in using my email and password
user_stories.benefit: so that I can access my personalized account dashboard and continue shopping.

user_stories.goal: I want to use the 'Forgot Password' option
user_stories.benefit: so that I can regain access to my account if I forget my password.

acceptance_criteria.description: Given valid credentials (email and password), the user must be successfully logged in and receive a valid authentication token.
Given invalid credentials, the system must display a generic, clear error message without revealing whether the email or password was incorrect.
Given the user initiates the 'Forgot Password' flow, the system must send a unique, time-limited password reset link to the registered email address.

output_requirements.name: Authentication Token
output_requirements.description: A JSON Web Token (JWT) containing user identification and expiration details.

output_requirements.name: Error Message
output_requirements.description: A structured error response detailing the failure reason (e.g., 'Invalid credentials').

api_expectations.description: Handles user login and returns JWT.
Initiates the password reset process by sending an email.

data_requirements.description: The securely hashed version of the user's password.

validation_rules.description: Email format must adhere to standard RFC 5322 guidelines.
Password must meet minimum complexity requirements (e.g., minimum 8 characters, mix of cases, numbers).

constraints.description: The application must utilize the MERN stack (MongoDB, Express, React, Node.js).
Authentication must be managed using JSON Web Tokens (JWT) for stateless session management.
The backend logic must adhere to the Model-View-Controller (MVC) architectural pattern.

assumptions.description: The user account already exists in the system database.
The system has access to a reliable email service provider (ESP) for password reset functionality.

assumptions: Revision requested by human_user: Add forgot password requirement and related acceptance criteria.
Fallback revision was used because LLM revision failed: Missing required SRS keys: ['traceability']

risks.description: Man-in-the-Middle (MITM) attacks could intercept credentials. Mitigation requires enforcing HTTPS/SSL.

risks: This fallback revision should be reviewed carefully before approval.

dependencies.description: Requires a functional user database schema capable of storing hashed passwords and unique emails.

revision_metadata.revision_comment: Add forgot password requirement and related acceptance criteria.
